title: rhamnus lycioides subsp. atlantica
intro: Rhamnus lycioides subsp. atlantica, also known as the Atlantic buckthorn, is a subspecies of the Rhamnus lycioides shrub native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rhamnus lycioides subsp. atlantica include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments.
